---
title: "Group project"
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(caret)
```

```{r}
Stock_Market_Dataset <- read.csv("Stock Market Dataset.csv")

View(Stock_Market_Dataset)
```

```{r}
class(Stock_Market_Dataset$Date)
#Stock_Market_Dataset$Date <-dmy(Stock_Market_Dataset$Date)
#Stock_Market_Dataset$Bitcoin_Price <- as.numeric(Stock_Market_Dataset$Bitcoin_Price)
class(Stock_Market_Dataset$Apple_Price)
class(Stock_Market_Dataset$Apple_Vol.)
```

```{r}
Stock_Market_Dataset %>%
  select() %>%
  na.omit
```

```{r}
str(Stock_Market_Dataset)
```

```{r}
Stock_Market_Dataset %>%
ggplot(aes(x = Date, y = Apple_Vol., group = 1)) +
  geom_line()

```

```{r}
Stock_Market_Dataset2 %>%
  ggplot(aes(Apple_Vol.)) +
  geom_histogram() 
ggplot(Stock_Market_Dataset2)+
  geom_histogram(aes(Apple_Price))
ggplot(Stock_Market_Dataset)+
  geom_histogram(aes(Crude_oil_Price))
ggplot(Stock_Market_Dataset)+
  geom_histogram(aes(Crude_oil_Vol.))
```

```{r}
Stock_Market_Dataset2 <- Stock_Market_Dataset[Stock_Market_Dataset$Crude_oil_Price >= 0, ]
```

```{r}
less <- which(Stock_Market_Dataset$Apple_Price < 79.5)
more <- which(Stock_Market_Dataset$Apple_Price > 162.91)
#mid <- which(is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset$Apple_Price))
Stock_Market_Dataset$Applethreshold[less] = "Low price"
#Stock_Market_Dataset$Applethreshold[mid] = "Medium price"
Stock_Market_Dataset$Applethreshold[more] = "High price"

Stock_Market_Dataset$Applethreshold<- replace_na(Stock_Market_Dataset$Applethreshold, "Medium Price")
view(Stock_Market_Dataset)
```

```{r}
Stock_Market_Dataset %>%
ggplot() +
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(Applethreshold))
```

```{r}
#check for missing values in columns
print("Bitcoin price missing values")
sum(is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset$Bitcoin_Price))
print("bitcoin vol missing values")
sum(is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset$Bitcoin_Vol.))
print("ether price missing values")
sum(is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset$Ethereum_Price))
print("ether vol missing values")
sum(is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset$Ethereum_Vol.))
print("natural gas price missing values")
sum(is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset$Natural_Gas_Price))
print("natural gas vol missing values")
sum(is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset$Natural_Gas_Vol.))
print("crude oil price missing values")
sum(is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset$Crude_oil_Price))
print("crude oil vol missing values")
sum(is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset$Crude_oil_Vol.))
print("meta price missing values")
sum(is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset$Meta_Price))
print("meta vol missing values")
sum(is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset$Meta_Vol.))
print("google price missing values")
sum(is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset$Google_Price))
print("google vol missing values")
sum(is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset$Google_Vol.))
print("apple price missing values")
sum(is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset$Apple_Price))
print("apple vol missing values")
sum(is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset$Apple_Vol.))
print("s.p 500 missing values")
sum(is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset$S.P_500_Price))
print("Nasdaq 100 price missing values")
sum(is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset$Nasdaq_100_Price))
print("nasaq 100 vol missing values")
sum(is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset$Nasdaq_100_Vol.))
```

```{r}
#sapply(Stock_Market_Dataset, summary)
print("")
summary(Stock_Market_Dataset$Apple_Price)
print("")
summary(Stock_Market_Dataset$Apple_Vol.)
print("")
summary(Stock_Market_Dataset$Crude_oil_Vol.)
print("")
summary(Stock_Market_Dataset$Crude_oil_Price)
```

```{r}
#Count outliers
Stock_Market_Dataset# %>% 
 # select(Crude_oil_Price)

outlier_apple <- length(boxplot.stats(Stock_Market_Dataset$Crude_oil_Price)$out)
view(outlier_apple)
```

```{r}
#counts how many outliers there are (hopefully)
outliers <- function(x){
outlier <- length(boxplot.stats(x)$out)
print(paste("outliers: ", outlier))
percent <- (outlier/sum(x, na.rm = TRUE))
print(paste("%", percent))
}
#USAGE: outliers(yourdataset$yourcolumn)
outliers(Stock_Market_Dataset$Apple_Price)
outliers(Stock_Market_Dataset$Apple_Vol.)
#outliers(Stock_Market_Dataset$Meta_Price)
#outliers(Stock_Market_Dataset$Meta_Vol.)
outliers(Stock_Market_Dataset$Crude_oil_Price)
outliers(Stock_Market_Dataset$Crude_oil_Vol.)
#sapply(Stock_Market_Dataset, outliers)

```

```{r}
Stock_Market_Dataset %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_boxplot(mapping = aes(y = Meta_Price))
```

```{r}
percent_missing <- function(x) {
  mean(is.na(x)) * 100
}

percent_outliers <- function(x) {
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  q1 <- quantile(x, 0.25)
  q3 <- quantile(x, 0.75)
  iqr <- q3 - q1
  lower <- q1 - 1.5 * iqr
  upper <- q3 + 1.5 * iqr
  mean(x < lower | x > upper) * 100
}

variables <- c("Apple_Price", "Apple_Vol.", "Crude_oil_Price", "Crude_oil_Vol.")

result <- data.frame(
  Variable = variables,
  Percent_Missing = sapply(Stock_Market_Dataset[ , variables], percent_missing),
  Percent_Outliers = sapply(Stock_Market_Dataset[ , variables], percent_outliers)
)
print(result)
```

```{r}
Stock_Market_Dataset2 <-
Stock_Market_Dataset[!is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset$Crude_oil_Vol.), ]

sum(is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Crude_oil_Vol))
view(Stock_Market_Dataset2)
```

```{r}
log_plot <- function(column_name, dataset) {
  plot_title <- paste("Log Transformation of", column_name)
  
  ggplot(dataset, aes_string(x = paste0("", column_name, ""))) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
    labs(title = plot_title, x = paste("", column_name, "", sep = ""), y = "count") +
    theme_minimal()
}

log_plot("Apple_Price", Stock_Market_Dataset2)
log_plot("Apple_Vol.", Stock_Market_Dataset2)
log_plot("Crude_oil_Price", Stock_Market_Dataset2)
log_plot("Crude_oil_Vol.", Stock_Market_Dataset2)
```

```{r}
Stock_Market_Dataset2$Year <- format(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Date, "%Y")
Stock_Market_Dataset2$Month <- format(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Date, "%m")

Apple_yearly_high <- aggregate(
  cbind(Apple_Price, Apple_Vol.) ~ Year,
  data = Stock_Market_Dataset2,
  FUN = max,
  na.rm = TRUE
)
Apple_yearly_low <- aggregate(
  cbind(Apple_Price, Apple_Vol.) ~ Year,
  data = Stock_Market_Dataset2,
  FUN = min,
  na.rm = TRUE
)
Apple_monthly_high <- aggregate(
  cbind(Apple_Price, Apple_Vol.) ~ Month,
  data = Stock_Market_Dataset2,
  FUN = max,
  na.rm = TRUE
)

Apple_monthly_Low <- aggregate(
  cbind(Apple_Price, Apple_Vol.) ~ Month,
  data = Stock_Market_Dataset2,
  FUN = min,
  na.rm = TRUE
)


Crude_oil_yearly_high <- aggregate(
  cbind(Crude_oil_Price, Crude_oil_Vol.) ~ Year,
  data = Stock_Market_Dataset2,
  FUN = max,
  na.rm = TRUE
)
Crude_oil_yearly_low <- aggregate(
  cbind(Crude_oil_Price, Crude_oil_Vol.) ~ Year,
  data = Stock_Market_Dataset2,
  FUN = min,
  na.rm = TRUE
)
Crude_oil_monthly_high <- aggregate(
  cbind(Crude_oil_Price, Crude_oil_Vol.) ~ Month,
  data = Stock_Market_Dataset2,
  FUN = max,
  na.rm = TRUE
)

Crude_oil_monthly_Low <- aggregate(
  cbind(Crude_oil_Price, Crude_oil_Vol.) ~ Month,
  data = Stock_Market_Dataset2,
  FUN = min,
  na.rm = TRUE
)
print(Apple_yearly_high)
print(Apple_yearly_low)
print(Apple_monthly_high)
print(Apple_monthly_Low)
print(Crude_oil_yearly_high)
print(Crude_oil_yearly_low)
print(Crude_oil_monthly_high)
print(Crude_oil_monthly_Low)
```

```{r}
ggplot() +
  geom_line(mapping = aes(x = Apple_yearly_high$Year, y = Apple_yearly_high$Apple_Price, group = 1)) +
  xlab("Year")+
  ylab("Price")
  ggplot() +
  geom_line(mapping = aes(x = Apple_yearly_low$Year, y = Apple_yearly_low$Apple_Price, group = 1)) +
xlab("Year")+
  ylab("Price")
ggplot() +
  geom_line(mapping = aes(x = Apple_monthly_high$Month, y = Apple_monthly_high$Apple_Price, group = 1)) +
xlab("Month")+
  ylab("Price")
ggplot() +
  geom_line(mapping = aes(x = Apple_monthly_Low$Month, y = Apple_monthly_Low$Apple_Price, group = 1)) +
xlab("Month")+
  ylab("Price")
```

```{r}
ggplot(Stock_Market_Dataset2, aes(x = Apple_Price, y = Apple_Vol.)) +
  geom_point(color = "purple") +
  geom_smooth(color = "darkblue") +
  xlab("Price")+
  ylab("Volume")
```

```{r}
ggplot(Stock_Market_Dataset2, aes(x = Crude_oil_Price, y = Crude_oil_Vol.)) +
  geom_point(color = "red") +
  geom_smooth(color = "black") +
  xlab("Price")+
  ylab("Volume")
```

```{r}
ggplot(Stock_Market_Dataset2, aes(x = Stock_Market_Dataset2$Date)) +
  geom_point(aes(y = Apple_Price, color = "apple")) +
  geom_point(aes(y = Meta_Price, color = "meta") ) +
  xlab("Date")+
  ylab("Price") +
  labs(color = "Apple_Price")
```

```{r}
#basic cor.test for each possible tech sector stock
#creates cor test function USAGE "cortest(df$x, df$y)"
cortest <- function(x, y) {
  
corresult <- cor.test(x, y)
corresult
}

print("Prices")
print("Apple, Meta")
cortest(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Apple_Price, Stock_Market_Dataset2$Meta_Price)

print("Apple, Google")
cortest(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Apple_Price, Stock_Market_Dataset2$Google_Price)

print("Google, Meta")
cortest(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Google_Price, Stock_Market_Dataset2$Meta_Price)

print("Volume")
print("Apple, Meta")
cortest(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Apple_Vol., Stock_Market_Dataset2$Meta_Vol.)

print("Apple, Google")
cortest(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Apple_Vol., Stock_Market_Dataset2$Google_Vol.)

print("Google, Meta")
cortest(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Google_Vol., Stock_Market_Dataset2$Meta_Vol.)

print("Price vs Volume")
print("Apple")
cortest(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Apple_Price, Stock_Market_Dataset2$Apple_Vol.)

print("Google")
cortest(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Google_Vol., Stock_Market_Dataset2$Google_Price)

print("Meta")
cortest(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Meta_Vol., Stock_Market_Dataset2$Meta_Price)
```

```{r}
ggplot(Stock_Market_Dataset2, aes(Apple_Price, Meta_Price)) +
  geom_point() +
  stat_smooth(method = lm)

ggplot(Stock_Market_Dataset2, aes(Apple_Price, Google_Price)) +
  geom_point() +
  stat_smooth(method = lm)

ggplot(Stock_Market_Dataset2, aes(Google_Price, Meta_Price)) +
  geom_point() +
  stat_smooth(method = lm)

ggplot(Stock_Market_Dataset2, aes(Apple_Vol., Meta_Vol.)) +
  geom_point() +
  stat_smooth(method = lm)

ggplot(Stock_Market_Dataset2, aes(Apple_Vol., Google_Vol.)) +
  geom_point() +
  stat_smooth(method = lm)

ggplot(Stock_Market_Dataset2, aes(Google_Vol., Meta_Vol.)) +
  geom_point() +
  stat_smooth(method = lm)

ggplot(Stock_Market_Dataset2, aes(Apple_Price, Apple_Vol.)) +
  geom_point() +
  stat_smooth(method = lm)

ggplot(Stock_Market_Dataset2, aes(Google_Vol., Google_Price)) +
  geom_point() +
  stat_smooth(method = lm)

ggplot(Stock_Market_Dataset2, aes(Meta_Vol., Meta_Price)) +
  geom_point() +
  stat_smooth(method = lm)
```

```{r}
print("Natural gas price, Crude oil price")
cortest(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Natural_Gas_Price, Stock_Market_Dataset2$Crude_oil_Price)

print("Natural gas vol, Crude oil vol")
cortest(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Natural_Gas_Vol., Stock_Market_Dataset2$Crude_oil_Vol.)

print("Crude oil price and vol")
cortest(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Crude_oil_Price, Stock_Market_Dataset2$Crude_oil_Vol.)

print("Natural gas price and vol")
cortest(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Natural_Gas_Price, Stock_Market_Dataset2$Natural_Gas_Vol.)
```

```{r}
#Stock_Market_Dataset2 %>%
#tech_cuts <- cut(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Apple_Price, breaks = quantile(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Apple_price))
#tech_cuts
sm <- as.numeric(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Apple_Price)
quartiles <- quantile(sm, prob = c(.25, .5, .75), na.rm = TRUE)
quantile(sm)
tech_cuts <- cut(sm, breaks = quantile(sm))
tech_cuts
```

```{r}
sm <- as.numeric(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Apple_Price)
quartiles <- quantile(sm, prob = c(.25, .5, .75), na.rm = TRUE)
Stock_Market_Dataset2 <- Stock_Market_Dataset2 %>%
mutate(
    apple_price_category2 = cut(
      Stock_Market_Dataset2$Apple_Price,
      breaks = c(-Inf, quartiles, Inf),
      labels = c("Bottom 25%", "25%-50%", "50%-75%", "Top 25%"),
      include.lowest = TRUE
    )
  )
#view(Stock_Market_Dataset2)
data.frame(
  price = Stock_Market_Dataset2$Apple_Price, 
  category = Stock_Market_Dataset2$apple_price_category
)
```

```{r}
#Ditto from above except its an easier to use function
#USAGE: create.quantiles(yourdataset$column, "Name of new column in quotes")
create.quantiles <- function(x, y) {
sm <- as.numeric(x)
quartiles <- quantile(sm, prob = c(.25, .5, .75), na.rm = TRUE)
Stock_Market_Dataset2 <- Stock_Market_Dataset2 %>%
mutate(
    meta_price_category = cut(
      x,
      breaks = c(-Inf, quartiles, Inf),
      labels = c("Bottom 25%", "25%-50%", "50%-75%", "Top 25%"),
      include.lowest = TRUE
    )
  )
data.frame(
  price = x, 
  category = (Stock_Market_Dataset2$y)
)
}
#create.quantiles(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Apple_Price, "apple_price_category")
create.quantiles(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Meta_Price)#, "meta_price_category")
view(Stock_Market_Dataset2)

```

```{r}
result <- lm(Apple_Price ~ Apple_Vol., data = Stock_Market_Dataset2)
print("Predict Apple_Price using Apple_vol.")
summary(result)
result <- lm(Meta_Price ~ Meta_Vol., data = Stock_Market_Dataset2)
print("Predict Meta_Price using Meta_vol.")
summary(result)
result <- lm(Google_Price ~ Google_Vol., data = Stock_Market_Dataset2)
print("Predict Google_Price using Google_vol.")
summary(result)
```

```{r}
#create a new dataset consisting of tech stocks using select
tech.stock <- select(Stock_Market_Dataset2, Apple_Price, Apple_Vol., Meta_Price, Meta_Vol., Google_Price, Google_Vol.)
```

```{r}
#Trying to split dataset into testing and training sets

set.seed(101)
#apple price split
sample <- tech.stock$Apple_Price %>% createDataPartition(p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
atrain <- tech.stock[sample, ]
atest <- tech.stock[-sample, ]
atrain
atest
#Meta price split
sample <- tech.stock$Meta_Price %>% createDataPartition(p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
mtrain <- tech.stock[sample, ]
mtest <- tech.stock[-sample, ]
mtrain
mtest
#Google price split
sample <- tech.stock$Google_Price %>% createDataPartition(p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
gtrain <- tech.stock[sample, ]
gtest <- tech.stock[-sample, ]
gtrain
gtest
```

```{r}
#apple price primer
aprice_lm <- lm(Apple_Price ~ ., data = atrain)

summary(aprice_lm)
```

```{r}
#Meta price primer
mprice_lm <- lm(Meta_Price ~ ., data = mtrain)

summary(mprice_lm)
```

```{r}
#Google price primer
gprice_lm <- lm(Google_Price ~ ., data = gtrain)

summary(gprice_lm)
```

```{r}
confint(aprice_lm)
confint(mprice_lm)
confint(gprice_lm)
```

```{r}
#apple price predict
apredictions <- aprice_lm %>% predict(atest)

head(apredictions)
```

```{r}
#Meta price predict
mpredictions <- mprice_lm %>% predict(mtest)

head(mpredictions)
```

```{r}
#Google price predict
gpredictions <- gprice_lm %>% predict(gtest)

head(gpredictions)
```

```{r}
#apple price actual vs predicted
aresults_df <- data.frame(
  Actual = atest$Apple_Price,
  Predicted = apredictions
)
head(aresults_df)

aresults_df %>% ggplot(aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted)) +
  geom_point(color = "blue", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted Apple Price vs Actual Values",
    x = "Actual Values",
    y = "Predicted Values"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
rmse_val <- RMSE(aresults_df$Predicted, aresults_df$Actual)
print("rmse")
print(rmse_val)
```

```{r}
#meta price actual vs predicted
mresults_df <- data.frame(
  Actual = mtest$Meta_Price,
  Predicted = mpredictions
)
head(mresults_df)

mresults_df %>% ggplot(aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted)) +
  geom_point(color = "blue", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted Meta Price vs Actual Values",
    x = "Actual Values",
    y = "Predicted Values"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
rmse_val <- RMSE(mresults_df$Predicted, mresults_df$Actual)
print("rmse")
print(rmse_val)
```

```{r}
#Google price actual vs predicted
gresults_df <- data.frame(
  Actual = gtest$Google_Price,
  Predicted = gpredictions
)
head(gresults_df)

gresults_df %>% ggplot(aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted)) +
  geom_point(color = "blue", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted Google price vs Actual Values",
    x = "Actual Values",
    y = "Predicted Values"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
rmse_val <- RMSE(gresults_df$Predicted, gresults_df$Actual)
print("rmse")
print(rmse_val)
```

```{r}

Mean_apple = mean(tech.stock$Apple_Price, na.rm = TRUE)
    SD_apple = sd(tech.stock$Apple_Price, na.rm = TRUE)
Mean_meta = mean(tech.stock$Meta_Price, na.rm = TRUE)
    SD_meta = sd(tech.stock$Meta_Price, na.rm = TRUE)
Mean_google = mean(tech.stock$Google_Price, na.rm = TRUE)
    SD_google = sd(tech.stock$Google_Price, na.rm = TRUE)
```

```{r}
tech.stock <- tech.stock %>%
  mutate(
    Apple_Classification = case_when(
      Apple_Price < (Mean_apple - SD_apple) ~ "Low",
      Apple_Price > (Mean_apple + SD_apple) ~ "High",
      TRUE ~ "Medium"
    )
  )

tech.stock <- tech.stock %>%
  mutate(
    Meta_Classification = case_when(
      Meta_Price < (Mean_meta - SD_meta) ~ "Low",
      Meta_Price > (Mean_meta + SD_meta) ~ "High",
      TRUE ~ "Medium"
    )
  )

tech.stock <- tech.stock %>%
  mutate(
    Google_Classification = case_when(
      Google_Price < (Mean_google - SD_google) ~ "Low",
      Google_Price > (Mean_google + SD_google) ~ "High",
      TRUE ~ "Medium"
    )
  )
view(tech.stock)
```

```{r}
tech.stock$Apple_Binary <- ifelse(tech.stock$Apple_Classification == "High", 1, 0)
tech.stock$Meta_Binary <- ifelse(tech.stock$Meta_Classification == "High", 1, 0)
tech.stock$Google_Binary <- ifelse(tech.stock$Google_Classification == "High", 1, 0)

view(tech.stock)

```

```{r}
class_model <- glm(tech.stock$Apple_Binary ~ ., data = train, family = "binomial")
summary(class_model)
```

```{r}
data.frame(tech.stock$Apple_Binary)
data.frame(tech.stock$Apple_Price)
data.frame(tech.stock$Meta_Price)
data.frame(tech.stock$Meta_Vol.)
data.frame(tech.stock$Google_Price)
data.frame(tech.stock$Google_Vol.)
data.frame(tech.stock$Apple_Classification)
data.frame(tech.stock$Apple_Binary)
data.frame(tech.stock$Meta_Classification)
data.frame(tech.stock$Meta_Binary)
data.frame(tech.stock$Google_Classification)
data.frame(tech.stock$Google_Binary)
```
